name: Lü

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Lü (luuu1242).

notes: Tai (Southwestern Tai) language of Sipsong Panna and neighbouring areas, written with the New Tai Lue script. New Tai Lue is an abugida with high- and low-class consonant series, an inherent vowel, separately placed vowel signs and explicit tone letters, so the phonetic value of a consonant symbol depends on its class and the accompanying tone marking rather than being fixed. Reading a spelling as IPA requires a per-word pronunciation lexicon that the engine does not bundle; grapheme->IPA coverage is lexicon-dependent and effectively zero without that stratum, and no letter->IPA table is asserted. Lü is tonal, and tone is left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Lü (luuu1242)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
